model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the button in the top-left corner
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (308,151) said dx=-455 dy=-470 conf=0.80 -> (-147,-319)
  iter 1: at (0,0) said dx=45 dy=30 conf=0.90 -> (45,30)
  iter 2: at (22,15) said dx=28 dy=20 conf=0.90 -> (50,35)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to act as a DVOC verifier to click a button in the top-left corner.
The image shows a red crosshair and a target button (a white rectangle).
The red crosshair is currently at (50, 35) based on the previous iterations provided in the text.
Wait, the previous iterations say:
- iter 0: at (308, 151), dx=-455, dy=-470 -> (-147, -319)
- iter 1: at (0, 0), dx=45, dy=30 -> (45, 30)
- iter 2: at (22, 15), dx=28, dy=20 -> (50, 35)
So current position is (50, 35).
Looking at the image:
The red crosshair is centered on the white rectangle button.
The button is in the top-left corner.
The crosshair looks very close to the center of the button.
Let's look closer at the crop.
The red crosshair center is exactly on the center of the white rectangle.
The target element is the white button.
The crosshair is already on it.
dx = 0, dy = 0. Confidence = 1.0.
